mnemonics: PIE: Principles of Inheritance, Encapsulation, and Abstraction
keyPoints: Encapsulation: Bundling data and methods into a single unit (class) and restricting access to the inner workings.
Inheritance: Creating new classes (child classes) that acquire properties and behaviors of existing classes (parent classes).
Polymorphism: Allowing objects of different classes to be treated as objects of a common super class, enabling one interface for multiple forms.
commonMistakes: Confusing Inheritance (is-a relationship) with Composition (has-a relationship).
Making class fields public instead of private, breaking the principle of Encapsulation.
Attempting to access a subclass-specific method using a superclass reference variable without proper casting.
